title: SwitchBot’s Standing Circulator Fan is worth fighting for
summary: The SwitchBot Standing Circulator Fan is a battery-powered 3D fan that tilts in multiple directions to circulate air throughout a room, featuring a quiet motor and multi-hour battery life. The device can convert between desktop and standing modes and includes an integrated nightlight, while supporting smart home integration, making it a compelling alternative to traditional fan options.
content:
I’m a fan.	

I can't remember the last time I got excited about a fan. Normally, I just buy whatever Vornado or Dreo model fits my budget, but that was before I started testing the battery-powered Standing Circulator Fan from SwitchBot.
As the name indicates, the SwitchBot fan is a 3D circulator - a fancy way of saying that it can tilt up, down, left, and right to push a decent amount air around a room. It looks okay, despite all the plastic, is relatively quiet, runs for hours on battery, has an integrated nightlight, transforms from a desktop to standing fan in seconds, and works on its own or as part of a smart home. There's a lot to like here.
Over …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: SwitchBot直立循环扇值得入手
zh.summary: SwitchBot直立循环扇是一款电池驱动的3D风扇，可向多个方向倾斜以在房间中循环空气，配备安静马达和超长电池续航。该设备可在桌面和直立模式间切换，内置夜灯功能，支持智能家居集成，是传统风扇的绝佳替代方案。

title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he’s doing that for robots.
summary: Jean-Baptiste Kempf, known for his open-source work on video players, is launching Kyber, an infrastructure platform for real-time remote device control. This represents his expansion from consumer media software to robotics infrastructure.
content: French serial entrepreneur and open-source legend Jean-Baptiste Kempf has been building Kyber, an infrastructure layer to control remote devices in real time.

zh.title: 他让你的免费视频播放器运行流畅。现在他正为机器人做同样的事。
zh.summary: Jean-Baptiste Kempf以其在开源视频播放器方面的工作而著称,现在正推出Kyber,这是一个用于实时远程设备控制的基础设施平台。这代表了他从消费者媒体软件向机器人基础设施的扩展。
